message:
perf(search/canonicalize): cache DefaultCanonicalizer in thread-local

canonicalize_for_embedding was calling DefaultCanonicalizer::default()
on every invocation. The struct is a stateless POD (three usize
thresholds) so ::default() is cheap in isolation, but at the volume
the embedding pipeline calls this function (once per snippet per
index pass) even the constructor register-setup + field-zeroing
showed up as non-zero on the embedding critical path.

New shape:

    thread_local! {
        static CANONICALIZER: DefaultCanonicalizer = DefaultCanonicalizer::default();
    }

    pub fn canonicalize_for_embedding(text: &str) -> String {
        CANONICALIZER.with(|c| c.canonicalize(text))
    }

Thread-local (not OnceLock) because DefaultCanonicalizer may grow
non-Send state later (e.g. per-thread buffers for
input-length-short-circuiting). Moving to a global OnceLock would
lock us out of that evolution.

Behaviour unchanged: DefaultCanonicalizer::canonicalize() is a
by-&self method with no interior mutability today, so the thread-
local is effectively a free copy.
